title: External Feedback Triage / Public Issue Hygiene
context: GrantLayer is publicly readable on GitHub in a developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture following GL-176 through GL-182. This artifact defines the lightweight public triage model for early external feedback.

feedback_categories.description: Something prevents the reviewer from completing any task
feedback_categories.typical_handling: fix now or route to urgent follow-up

feedback_categories.description: Documentation is unclear, contradictory, or hard to follow
Clone/run path or first verifiable output does not work

feedback_categories.typical_handling: add to backlog or fix now if it blocks comprehension

feedback_categories.description: The reviewer is asking what GrantLayer does or how it works
feedback_categories.typical_handling: answer publicly if safe and concise

feedback_categories.description: Potential security issue or sensitive reporting concern
Concern about production suitability, support, or operational maturity
Valid feedback that is out of scope for the current developer-preview posture

proposed_labels.name: feedback
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Any external reviewer feedback that is not a bug/security report
proposed_labels.safety_notes: Keep wording neutral; do not imply support commitment

proposed_labels.name: good-first-feedback
proposed_labels.purpose: Friendly entry point for low-risk reviewer input
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Small docs notes, simple quickstart comments, or minor wording suggestions
proposed_labels.safety_notes: Do not use for security issues or production commitments

proposed_labels.name: docs
proposed_labels.when_to_use: README, SECURITY, quickstart, examples, or guide clarity

proposed_labels.name: quickstart
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Anything affecting the first verifiable output path

proposed_labels.name: bug
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Actual broken behavior that is not a security concern
proposed_labels.safety_notes: If security-sensitive, do not use this label publicly

proposed_labels.name: security-review-needed
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Any report that may involve secrets, exploit details, or exposure concerns
proposed_labels.safety_notes: Direct to GitHub Security Advisories; do not ask for exploit steps publicly

proposed_labels.name: production-readiness
proposed_labels.purpose: Feedback about production suitability or caveats
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Questions or concerns about production status, SLA, onboarding, or operational maturity
proposed_labels.safety_notes: Do not let this become a promise of production readiness

proposed_labels.name: question
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Reviewer asks what GrantLayer is or how a documented feature works

proposed_labels.name: enhancement
proposed_labels.when_to_use: New capability request that is not a bug

proposed_labels.name: external-review
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Any issue that came from an invited external reviewer

proposed_labels.name: needs-triage
proposed_labels.when_to_use: New feedback that has not yet been categorized and severity-assigned

proposed_labels.name: blocked
proposed_labels.when_to_use: Missing reproduction, unclear report, or awaiting safer handling
proposed_labels.safety_notes: Do not use to silence valid feedback

severity_mapping.criteria: Serious issue affecting safety or first-use trust

intake_workflow: decide public issue vs private security advisory
decide next action: fix now, clarify docs, add to backlog, defer, close as non-scope, escalate security

rules: Do not post exploit details publicly.
Do not post customer data publicly.
Use GitHub Security Advisories for security-sensitive reports.
Public issues should use minimal reproduction with synthetic data only.
If a report includes a secret, treat it as an incident or rotation concern, not normal feedback.
If a report suggests a repository leak, handle it as a security and hygiene matter before any broader public response.

issue_template_decision.rationale: The label and routing model is the higher priority for the first external review window, and existing public templates already cover basic bug and security pathways.
issue_template_decision.templates_deferred_reason: Early feedback should be observed before locking the repository into a more rigid public issue template taxonomy.
issue_template_decision.later_issue_recommendation: Add a minimal feedback template after the first reviewer window confirms the category and label set.

non_goals: No collection of real grant or customer data.
No automated GitHub label creation in this issue.
No public GitHub push in this issue.
No visibility change in this issue.
No internal repo push directly to GitHub in this issue.

findings.recommendation: Keep GL-183 documentation-only and defer issue-template expansion until the first external review window is complete.

next_recommended_step: GL-184 First External Reviewer Invite Pack
